Advancing Your Career This Weekend

For many technology professionals, the weekend provides a strategic opportunity to focus on career progression; Whether updating resumes, researching new roles, or preparing targeted applications for the week ahead.

At OperAxis, we recognize this commitment and specialize in accelerating successful placements for U.S. technology talent. Through our Placement-as-a-Service model, we support US Citizens, Green Card holders, OPT/CPT, and H-1B professionals with expert resume optimization, targeted interview preparation, immigration readiness assistance, and direct placement into contract and full-time roles aligned with major U.S. time zones (EST, CST, MT, PT).

We are actively placing qualified candidates into high-impact opportunities across the Energy, Financial Services, Automotive, and other enterprise sectors, including the following:

• Power BI Developer (GCP, BigQuery, DAX, Power Query) – Atlanta, GA (5 days onsite) 
• Scrum Master – New York, NY area (hybrid, 3 days onsite) 
• Application Development Program Manager – Alpharetta, GA area (hybrid, 3 days onsite) 
• Network Security DevOps Engineer (Fortinet, Automation, Cloud Security) – Spring, TX (100% onsite) 
• Enterprise Identity Engineer (Active Directory, PKI, Tier 0) – Spring, TX (100% onsite) 
• Full Stack Developers (Python + React, .NET with AI/LLM, Java + Angular/React + GCP) – multiple locations 
• Generative AI / LLM Senior Specialist – Dearborn, MI area (4 days onsite) 
• SAP S/4HANA Functional Lead (Order-to-Cash) – Dearborn, MI area (4 days onsite) 
• Data Engineer (GCP, BigQuery, Data Pipelines) – Dearborn, MI area (4 days onsite)

If you are actively pursuing new opportunities this weekend, we invite you to apply directly at; https://operaxis.net/jobs or submit your resume to; hr@operaxis.net. Please include your target role, preferred location, and any relevant details. Our team will conduct a prompt review and provide constructive, actionable feedback.

We look forward to partnering with you on your next career milestone.

👍 If you are job searching this weekend, or tag a colleague who may benefit from these opportunities.

Freelancing Opportunity | Hiring: Agentic Coding Annotator (Online / Offline Tasks)

Remote Freelancing Opportunity
Short-term contract (approx. 5 weeks)
Full-time commitment (8 hours/day)
Must be comfortable with overlapping work hours
Immediate start

We are looking for strong, detail-oriented software professionals to work on advanced AI model evaluation and coding tasks. This is not a basic annotation role—it requires solid engineering skills and the ability to read, debug, and validate code.

🔹 Roles & Responsibilities
Execute realistic coding tasks within an agent-based coding environment
Interact with models and evaluate their outputs without bias
Review and analyze model-generated code and solutions
Run commands, execute scripts, and interpret logs
Debug issues and validate correctness of implementations
Perform testing and manual verification of outputs
Rank and grade model responses based on structured evaluation criteria
Write clear, concise, and evidence-based justifications for evaluations

Review completed tasks for quality, completeness, and compliance
Identify gaps, broken environments, or unclear instructions and escalate with evidence

Strong experience in software engineering / coding roles (5+ years preferred)
Hands-on expertise in Python, JavaScript, Java, C/C++, SQL, or similar
Ability to read unfamiliar codebases and debug issues
Experience with CLI/Terminal, Git, and development tools
Strong experience in AI and Data Annotation
Understanding of LLM evaluation / AI model outputs / annotation workflows
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Ability to follow strict guidelines and maintain consistency

🧑‍💼 Role: Network Wireless Engineer with Aruba ClearPass
📍 Location: Down State, NY (Onsite)
🕒 Type: Contract

Total Experience: Must have 15+ Years

As a Java Developer you will:
•  Specialist Wireless Network Engineer required for a programme of work within team which aims to modernize and enhance the US network infrastructure to improve reliability, security, and user experience. Key initiatives include replacing end-of-life network hardware, deploying enhanced Wi-Fi solutions, and provisioning high-speed internet connectivity services overlayed with SDWAN technology.
•  The programme will also progress alternative connectivity options such as satellite and 5G solutions for disaster recovery and rapid deployment, while supporting facility expansion projects.
•  Comprehensive wireless surveys and design work will be a significant part of this engagement, with ad-hoc travel required to the US national grid sites to conduct physical Wi-Fi surveys and Desktop surveys with Ekahau and/or Hamina tools.
 
Key Responsibilities
•  Design, deploy and optimise enterprise wireless LAN solutions across office and industrial environments.
•  Conduct wireless surveys (predictive & on‑site), RF analysis and coverage optimisation.
•  Lead troubleshooting for complex Wi‑Fi issues, including authentication, interference and performance degradation.
•  Create and update Low‑Level Designs (LLDs) for Wi‑Fi, NAC and related network services.
•  Produce accurate Bill of Materials (BoM) for new deployments, refresh programmes and upgrade initiatives.
•  Support Wi‑Fi lifecycle management including hardware upgrades, software updates and capacity planning.
 
Required Skills & Experience
•  Strong hands‑on experience with enterprise WLAN technologies (Cisco, Aruba).
•  Must have : Strong Experience with Aruba ClearPass for 802.1X, RADIUS, profiling and role-based access
 
Deep understanding of:
•  RF fundamentals (channel planning, power, interference mitigation) 802.11 standards including Wi‑Fi 5/6/6E/7 WLAN authentication (EAP methods, RADIUS, role‑based access) Enterprise controllers, cloud‑managed Wi‑Fi platforms and policy engines
•  Proficiency with wireless survey tools (Ekahau, Hamina etc.)
•  Experience with Cradlepoint, or similar 5G cellular‑enabled networking platforms
•  Experience supporting large‑scale Wi‑Fi environments in complex organisations.
•  Experience with Aruba ClearPass for 802.1X, RADIUS, profiling and role-based access
•  Able to travel ad-hoc to National Grid locations for onsite pre and post deployment Wi-Fi surveys
 
Desirable Certifications
CWNP (CWNA, CWDP, CWSP, CWNE)
Cisco Wireless (CCNP/CCIE Wireless)
Aruba Expert (ACMP, ACDP, ACCX)

Job Role : Data Scientist

Location : Austin TX (Locals only )

Duration : 6+ months

Job Description:

"Resource with strong skills in Data architecture and expertise in dimensional data modeling, preferably in a bigdata or an enterprise data warehouse environment. Experience with Informatica Power Exchange for CDC based replication or similar tool is a plus. Expertise in development of data ingestion to Hadoop based data lake (HDFS, Hive) and S3 storage, complex data transformations, and data security. Programming expertise in a major programming/scripting language including but not limited to Java, C++, Scala, python, Go. Hands on delivery capability working in a team across the full lifecycle Data Source Analysis, Design, Development, Testing and Implementation. This includes providing input into requirements, platform development, technical design of the project level technical architecture for ETL, big data application design and development, testing, and deployment of the proposed solution. Proficient in Hadoop ecosystem components such as Hive, Yarn, Knox, Ranger, Sqoop, Oozie, HiveQL, Spark, SQL/data access. Experience in ETL development using Informatica BDM, Kafka, or comparable software. Understanding and skills in data governance, security architecture, load balancing and troubleshooting. H-1B Cap-Exempt

An H-1B cap-exempt petition enables an employer to sponsor a foreign national for an H-1B visa without being limited by the annual cap or required to go through the lottery process typically associated with standard H-1B filings.

How the H-1B Cap-Exempt Filing Process Works

1. Employer Eligibility Verification

The employer must confirm that they qualify as a cap-exempt organization (e.g., a non profit research institution, government research agency, or a non profit affiliated with a university).

2. File Labor Condition Application (LCA)

The employer submits a Labor Condition Application to the U.S. Department of Labor to ensure compliance with wage and working condition requirements.

3. Submit Form I-129

After the LCA is certified, the employer files Form I-129 (Petition for a Non immigrant Worker) with U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS).

4. Year-Round Processing

Since cap-exempt petitions are not subject to the H-1B annual quota, they can be submitted to USCIS at any time—no lottery or registration required.

5. Visa Stamping (If Outside the U.S.)

If the applicant is outside the United States, they must attend a consular interview to receive the H-1B visa stamp in their passport before entering the U.S.

Key Advantages of H-1B Cap-Exempt Sponsorship

No Lottery Required

Year-Round Submission

Faster, More Predictable Processing

Hiring:

Role: DCS IT Business Analyst 3 - 803022

Location: Atlanta, GA

Mode: C2C

Acts as the bridge between business and IT to design and implement system solutions. Analyzes complex needs, documents requirements, supports project rollouts, and provides advanced training/support to help users maximize functional business tools.

IN PERSON ONLY INTERVIEW - MUST BE A GEORGIA RESIDENT - NO OUT OF STATE APPLICANTS

Job Title: IT Business Analyst 3

Position Purpose

As an IT Business Analyst 3, you will serve as the strategic liaison between business units and IT departments to translate complex business needs into actionable, high-quality technical solutions. Under general supervision, you will lead the elicitation, analysis, and documentation of requirements for high-impact or enterprise-level systems. You will not only participate in the planning, design, and implementation of new or enhanced systems but will also act as a trusted advisor and mentor, driving operational efficiency and ensuring the user community fully leverages functional business tools and support systems.

Core Responsibilities

Requirement Elicitation & Analysis: Lead workshops, interviews, and focus groups with cross-functional stakeholders to extract, analyze, and prioritize complex business requirements.

Documentation & Design: Translate high-level business requirements into detailed functional specifications, user stories, use cases, workflow diagrams, and data models. Participate heavily in system design sessions to ensure technical solutions align with business goals.

Project Planning & Implementation: Partner with Project Managers and Technical Leads to define project scope, objectives, and deliverables. Coordinate and participate in the deployment, data migration, and rollout of new or enhanced applications.

Testing & Quality Assurance: Define User Acceptance Testing (UAT) strategies, write comprehensive test scenarios, and coordinate with business users to ensure deliverables meet validation criteria and quality standards.

User Support & Enablement: Serve as a subject matter expert (SME) for core functional business tools. Develop training materials, conduct user training sessions, and provide advanced Tier-2/Tier-3 support to resolve complex functional system issues.

Process Improvement: Continuously evaluate existing business processes and system workflows to identify bottlenecks, proposing innovative, automated, or optimized solutions.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

Advanced Analytical Thinking: Proven ability to synthesize complex, ambiguous data from multiple sources into clear, structured requirements.

Interested please share your resume to - Thendral@optimussinc.com

Job title : Android Developer

Location : Whippany, NJ

Duration : long term

Job Description:

An 8+ years experienced Android developer who designs, builds, and maintains applications for the Android platform. Translate UI/UX wireframes into functional, clean code, integrate APIs, and ensure app performance across multiple devices. Their goal is to deliver robust, secure, and user-friendly mobile experiences.

Core Responsibilities

· App Development: Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code using modern languages like Kotlin or Java.

· UI/UX Implementation: Translate designs and wireframes into intuitive user interfaces.

· API Integration: Connect mobile applications to backend services and third-party APIs (e.g., REST, JSON).

· Testing & Debugging: Perform unit and instrumentation testing to identify edge cases, fix bugs, and ensure overall app stability.

· Performance Optimization: Monitor live app performance, resolve bottlenecks, and improve responsiveness.

· Maintenance: Continuously evaluate and implement new technologies, release periodic updates, and safeguard user data.

Typical Skill Requirements

· Programming: Proficiency in Kotlin (industry standard) or Java.

· Tools: Mastery of Android Studio and the Android SDK.

· Architecture: Familiarity with modern architecture patterns (e.g., MVVM, MVP) and Jetpack libraries.

· Databases: Experience with local storage solutions like Room or SQLite.

· Soft Skills: Strong problem-solving, attention to detail, and a collaborative mindset for cross-functional teamwork.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Developers rarely work in a vacuum. A typical Android role requires close daily collaboration with:

· UI/UX Designers: To align on visual assets, user flows, and interface layouts.

· Backend Developers: To coordinate data exchange and API endpoints.

· QA/Testing Teams: To ensure features meet quality standards before release.

· Product Managers: To prioritize features and ensure the app aligns with business goals.
